title: Biscutella atlantica
taxonomy: Biscutella atlantica is classified within the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This classification places it among other flowering plants that produce fruit in the form of a capsule. The genus Biscutella, to which Biscutella atlantica belongs, is a group of flowering plants native to the Mediterranean region. Biscutella atlantica is a rare species found in the Atlantic islands, and it is often associated with coastal areas. As part of the Brassicaceae family, Biscutella atlantica shares characteristics with other plants in this family, such as the presence of four-petaled flowers.

intro: Biscutella atlantica is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the Atlantic biscutella or Atlantic crowfoot. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its health benefits are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Biscutella atlantica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Biscutella atlantica is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
